# CartoBoost Agent Operating Guide

> CartoBoost is a temporal-spatial boosting toolbox with a Python API, CLI
> tooling, and a benchmark-driven validation pipeline.

Use this guide as the first read before making changes. Start in the relevant code component, then update contracts, then run the validation commands defined in AGENTS.

## Workspace Overview
- [Repository README](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/README.md): global project intent, quick workflow examples, and component-level capabilities.
- [Root AGENTS instructions](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/AGENTS.md): workspace development rules, validation commands, and release caveats.
- [Python project manifest](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/pyproject.toml): dependency groups and Python packaging settings.
- [Python environment lockfile](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/uv.lock): pinned dependency state for reproducible Python builds.
- [Docs home](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/index.md): documentation index and user-facing map.
- [Docusaurus config](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docusaurus.config.ts): site metadata, navbar/footer, redirects, strict link handling, and GitHub Pages base URL.
- [Docusaurus sidebar](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/sidebars.ts): task-oriented documentation navigation.
- [Docs homepage component](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/src/pages/index.tsx): branded React landing page for the docs site.
- [Modeling Lab page](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/src/pages/modeling-lab.tsx) and [client](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/src/components/ModelingLabClient/index.tsx): browser-local WebAssembly forecasting with Prophet-style piecewise modeling aliases and debugger plots over every numeric WASM component and historical component key, regression, graph/neural modeling, week/month January 2024 yellow taxi sample loading, and opt-in boosted-tree splitter visualization.
- [Docs theme stylesheet](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/src/css/custom.css): Docusaurus theme tokens and visual overrides.
- [Feature catalog](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/feature_catalog.md): complete public feature map across regression, forecasting, sequence utilities, graph, neural, sparse, artifact, CLI, and benchmark surfaces.
- [General utilities](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/general_utilities.md): examples and plotting snippets for sequence reference utilities, Kalman filters with smoothing, residual metrics, variance/gain/likelihood diagnostics, and interval distributions, plus ordinary kriging, intermittent-demand methods, and single-series forecast helpers.
- [Plotting](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/plotting.md): reusable visualization helpers for predicted-vs-actual diagnostics, residuals, metric comparisons, forecast lines, forecast components, seasonality curves, horizon metrics, backtest fold metrics, interval calibration, changepoints, cutoff predictions, Prophet-compatible plotting utilities matching the public `prophet.plot` 1.2.2 surface, static spatial plots, PyDeck route maps, diagnostic report bundles, browser-local Modeling Lab piecewise trend/seasonality debugger and splitter visualization, figure saving, and rendered taxi benchmark image examples.

## Core Components
- [Python package API](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/python/cartoboost): user-facing estimator, graph helpers, neural feature workflows, and utility functions.
- [CLI reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/cli.md): command behavior for `train`, `predict`, `eval`, and `inspect`.
- [Neural features guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/neural-features.md): ID embeddings, neural-augmented boosting, graph-aware fallback, and benchmark guidance.
- [Graph features guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/graph-features.md): node2vec, GraphSAGE, HeteroGraphSAGE, HinSAGE, directed source-target features, and graph feature bundle contracts.
- [Scripts folder](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/scripts): acceptance metrics and dataset generation utilities used for validation.

## Documentation Contracts
- [Docs index](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/index.md): entrypoint and topical structure.
- [Installation guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/installation.md): install paths and optional extras, including the `holidays` extra for country holiday calendars.
- [Getting started](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md): first run flow and local checks.
- [Feature catalog](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/feature_catalog.md): full capability map and links to detailed topic pages.
- [Python API reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/python-api.md): authoritative API contracts tied to scientific model choice.
- [CLI reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/cli.md): argument/flag behavior and output formats.
- [Python estimator guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/python-estimator.md): estimator usage and expected semantics.
- [Choose a model guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/model-types.md):
  task-first router for the tabular regressor, classifier, ranker, forecasting
  classes, CartoBoost lag forecasting, kriging, neural embedding models, graph
  models, and utilities.
- [Classification quickstart](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/classification-quickstart.md),
  [Ranking quickstart](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/ranking-quickstart.md),
  [Categorical features](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/categorical-features.md),
  and [Spatial CV best practices](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/spatial-cv-best-practices.md):
  v0.2 tabular modeling guides for classifier, ranker, categorical, and
  leakage-aware spatial validation workflows.
- [Forecasting model guides](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/index.md): model-specific examples for naive, seasonal naive, theta, ETS, ARIMA, AutoARIMA, Kalman, fixed intermittent-demand forecasters, piecewise linear seasonal forecasting with component decomposition, trend-belief adjustments, residual shock propagation, kriging, CartoBoost lag, N-BEATS/N-HiTS wrappers, and weighted ensembles; separate from the shared forecasting workflow docs.
- [AutoForecaster guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/auto-forecaster.md): full Rust-native auto-selector lifecycle, candidate roster, validation, gating, prediction, metadata, and Mermaid diagrams.
- [Naive and seasonal naive guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/naive-seasonal.md): baseline last-value and seasonal-repeat forecasting examples.
- [Theta guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/theta.md): manual theta, optimized theta, seasonal theta, and validation notes.
- [ETS guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/ets.md): additive ETS examples, parameters, smoothing components, and validation notes.
- [ARIMA and AutoARIMA guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/arima.md): fixed-order ARIMA, bounded AutoARIMA, deterministic taxi-lane visualization workflow, native GIL-release notes, and benchmark guidance.
- [Kalman guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/kalman.md): local-level, local-linear-trend, self-tuning AutoKalman forecasting examples, Matplotlib visual diagnostics, smoothing/filtering diagnostics, and variance-grid tuning notes.
- [Piecewise linear seasonal guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/piecewise-linear-seasonal.md): trend, automatic and explicit changepoints, Prophet-shaped holiday tables, optional country holiday calendars, prior-scale aliases normalized into native penalties, seasonality, event, regressor, trend-adjustment, residual-shock, interval, `history_components()` / `history_components_frame()` historical trend and seasonality diagnostics for rolling-origin holdouts, Prophet-compatible plotting parity, and component-decomposition examples.
- [Kriging guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/kriging.md): coordinate-aware panel forecasting examples, empirical/fitted variograms, drift/neighbor controls, cached all-neighbor scoring, and leave-one-out residual diagnostics.
- [CartoBoost lag guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/cartoboost-lag.md): global supervised lag forecasting examples and native feature support.
- [Weighted ensembles guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/ensembles.md): explicit fixed-weight native forecasting ensembles.
- [Forecasting](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/forecasting.md): `ForecastFrame`, duplicate timestamp aggregation with `sample_weight_col`, rolling-origin validation, metrics, artifact, CLI, reconciliation, interval, and evidence contracts.
- [CLI guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/cli.md): workflow-oriented CLI usage patterns.
- [Feature schema](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/feature_schema.md): data schema and validation contract.
- [Sparse features](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/sparse_features.md): sparse-set, H3, and S2 helper contracts; H3/S2 deterministic validation and row assembly are Rust-backed.
- [Model artifact format](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/model_artifact.md): serialization and compatibility assumptions.
- [Constraints](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/constraints.md): legal/algorithmic domain constraints.
- [Spatial modeling](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/spatial_modeling.md): modeling assumptions for route/time/geographic features.
- [Graph features](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/graph-features.md): node2vec, GraphSAGE, HeteroGraphSAGE, HinSAGE, directionality, and metapath contracts.
- [General utilities](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/general_utilities.md): reusable Rust-backed utilities independent of the regressor and forecasting frame APIs, including sequence reference continuation, reference-path inference, leakage-safe OOF row generation and checks, group summaries, and candidate blending.
- [Plotting](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/plotting.md): optional visualization diagnostics for already-computed CartoBoost predictions, forecast artifacts, component rows, seasonality curves, changepoints, cutoff predictions, benchmark metric rows, validation folds, interval calibration, Prophet-compatible plotting utilities matching the public `prophet.plot` 1.2.2 surface, static spatial plots, interactive PyDeck maps, report bundles, browser-local Modeling Lab splitter visualization, and rendered taxi benchmark image examples.
- [Benchmarks overview](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/benchmarks/index.md),
  [Benchmark methodology](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/benchmarks/methodology.md),
  [Forecasting benchmarks](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/benchmarks/forecasting.md),
  [NYC Taxi](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/benchmarks/nyc-taxi.md),
  [Model suite](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/benchmarks/model-suite.md),
  [Taxi zone acceptance](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/benchmarks/taxi-zone.md):
  expected evidence format, maintained NYC regression baseline artifacts with
  external baselines and repeated-run confidence intervals, maintained
  validation-search public tabular and graph diagnostic artifacts, maintained
  forecasting artifacts, CartoBoost piecewise local diagnostics, M4/M5/M6
  committed samples, M5 full-roster sample, M5/M6 `official_metrics` artifact
  sections, M6 training-window RPS calibration, full-run protocols, and metric
  definitions.

## Where to Edit by Change Type
- For model behavior changes: update [feature schema](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/feature_schema.md)
  + [tests](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/tests) as
  needed. Native categorical handling is shared across regressor, classifier,
  and ranker wrappers for pandas categorical/string/object columns or
  `FeatureKind.CATEGORICAL` / `FeatureKind.ORDINAL` schema entries, with
  stable one-hot, subset partition, ordinal, or smoothed target-stat mappings
  persisted in artifacts. Spatial validation helpers include
  `spatial_buffered_cv`, `spatial_grouped_cv`, and `environmental_blocked_cv`;
  diagnostics include logloss, ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, Brier score, ECE, NDCG, MAP,
  MRR, `residual_morans_i`, and `spatial_cv_gap`.
- For forecasting model surface changes: update [Forecasting](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/forecasting.md), the relevant [model guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/forecasting-models/index.md), [Feature catalog](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/feature_catalog.md), [Python API reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/python-api.md), and this file.
- For CLI behavior and I/O format changes: update [CLI guide](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/user-guide/cli.md), [CLI reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/cli.md), and integration tests under `../tests/integration`.
- For Python API changes: edit `python/cartoboost`, update [Python API reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/python-api.md), and add/adjust tests under `../tests/python`.
- For reusable utility changes such as sequence primitives, Kalman filters, kriging, or intermittent-demand helpers: update [General utilities](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/general_utilities.md), [Feature catalog](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/feature_catalog.md), and [Python API reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/python-api.md).
- For plotting API changes: update [Plotting](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/plotting.md), [Python API reference](https://github.com/TheCulliganMan/CartoBoost/blob/main/docs/reference/python-api.md), and tests under `../tests/python`.
- For benchmark or acceptance behavior changes: edit `scripts`, benchmark sources in `benches`, and benchmark docs under `docs/benchmarks`.
- The v0.2 modeling smoke benchmark runner is
  `scripts/run_v02_modeling_benchmarks.py`; it writes JSON, JSONL, and
  Markdown artifacts under `target/v02-benchmarks` by default and covers binary
  spatial classification, grouped ranking, native categorical versus one-hot,
  spatial leakage, regression speed, and unsupported export gates.

## Validation and Runtime Checks
- Run `uv run --group dev pre-commit run --all-files` for lint and format checks after source edits.
- Run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build` after docs-site config, React, or Markdown navigation changes.
- Run `uv run --group dev pytest` after changing Python APIs or estimator behavior.
- Use `just validate` for full local validation only when comprehensive smoke coverage is needed.

## Optional
- [llms.txt spec](https://llmstxt.org/index.md): authoritative markdown format this file follows.
